item.title: Sphingosine 1-Phosphate (S1P) Receptor Modulators as an Induction and Maintenance Therapy for Moderate to Severe Ulcerative Colitis: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials.

item.title: Evaluation of Injection Site Pain and Adherence in Patients Transitioning from a High to Low Volume Adalimumab Formulation (AVT02, Simlandi®) Across Multiple Indications (EASE PAIN).
item.description: Rheumatology and therapy - Injection site pain (ISP) is a frequent concern with subcutaneous adalimumab therapy and may negatively affect treatment adherence and patient satisfaction. AVT02 is a high-concentration, low-volum...

item.title: Harnessing Nanocarriers to Modulate Gut Inflammation: A New Era in IBD Management.
item.description: Recent advances in inflammation & allergy drug discovery - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a chronic inflammatory condition affecting the gastrointestinal tract, primarily including Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis. Current treatments rely heavil...

item.title: Why the Assay Matters in Model-Informed Precision Dosing: An Example of Ustekinumab in Crohn's Disease.

item.title: Tofacitinib safety in inflammatory bowel disease: a disproportionality analysis of the FDA adverse event reporting system.

item.title: First Reported Case of Upadacitinib-Associated Cytomegalovirus Viremia and Suspected Pneumonitis in Ulcerative Colitis.

item.title: Correction to: DOP055 Efficacy and Safety Up to 3 Years of Risankizumab Maintenance Treatment in Patients With Moderately to Severely Active Ulcerative Colitis: Interim Results from Phase 3 COMMAND Open-Label Extension Study.

item.title: Pharmacovigilance analysis of infliximab in inflammatory bowel disease: novel safety signals and sex-specific adverse event profiles from the FAERS database (2004-2024).
